name: bacolonyzer
documentation:
BaColonyzer is a software to quantify the cell density of bacterial cultures
from timecourse pictures. It uses image analysis tools to determine the fitness
of spotted cultures of bacteria grown on solid agar.

modules.name: bacolonyzer.utils
documentation: Functions to set the inputs that will be used.

functions.name: bacolonyzer.utils.summarise
documentation:
Read user input, set up flags for analysis, report on
options chosen and find files to be analysed.
Set input parameters for analysis

functions.name: bacolonyzer.utils.get_grid_format
documentation:
Check that the input variable --gridformat is correct.
Raise an error message if --gridformat is wrong.

Extract number of rows and columns of the grid by using --gridformat.

functions.name: bacolonyzer.utils.range_float
documentation: Check the float is in [0, 1] range.

modules.name: bacolonyzer.filesystem
documentation: Functions to prepare de directories and to obtain the files for analysis.

functions.name: bacolonyzer.filesystem.reference_info
documentation: Check if reference image exists and provide information to users.

functions.name: bacolonyzer.filesystem.get_directory
documentation: Get directory to work with.

functions.name: bacolonyzer.filesystem.arrange_directories
documentation: Creates directories to save output files.

functions.name: bacolonyzer.filesystem.get_all_images
documentation: Returns an ordered list of images paths present in a given directory.

functions.name: bacolonyzer.filesystem.get_images
documentation:
Get filenames for all images in working directory.
Returns a list of filenames to analyse.

functions.name: bacolonyzer.filesystem.get_file_name
documentation: Get file names from given path removing extension and directories.

functions.name: bacolonyzer.filesystem.save_output
documentation: Saves output.

classes.name: bacolonyzer.commands.abstract.AbstractCommand

methods.name: bacolonyzer.commands.abstract.AbstractCommand.register_arguments
documentation: Register command arguments into the given parser.

methods.name: bacolonyzer.commands.abstract.AbstractCommand.run
documentation: Run command logic.

Abstract class used to define shared logic for all commands in
bacolonyzer.

modules.name: bacolonyzer.commands.analyse
documentation: Definition of all commands available in BaColonyzer.

modules.name: bacolonyzer.commands.entrypoint
documentation: !/usr/bin/env python3

functions.name: bacolonyzer.commands.entrypoint.run
documentation: Entrypoint from the command line.

modules.name: bacolonyzer.commands.stabilize_images
documentation: Definition of all commands available in BaColonyzer.

functions.name: bacolonyzer.commands.stabilize_images.load_image_color_gray
documentation:
Read an original image of the time-series as well as the
black-and-white version of this image.

modules.name: bacolonyzer.image_processing
documentation:
Functions to get the agar and spot colors, to locate the grid, and to
calibrate the image colors according to a black and white reference picture.

functions.name: bacolonyzer.image_processing.get_agar_spot_color
documentation:
Get the color value of the agar and the spots.
Obtain the values by checking the largest two peaks in the color histogram.

functions.name: bacolonyzer.image_processing.get_position_grid
documentation:
Get the position of the grid in the image.
Use an artificial pattern and the normalized squared difference to find it.
Return the position of the grid and the size of the scaled the pattern.

functions.name: bacolonyzer.image_processing.get_mask
documentation: Perform dilatation of the spots (and therefore reduction of the agar).

functions.name: bacolonyzer.image_processing.calibration_maxmin
documentation:
Obtain minimum and maximum intensity values captured by the camera by
using a reference image. This will be used to calibrate the final results.

functions.name: bacolonyzer.image_processing.show_grid_result
documentation: Create a new image showing the best grid match and the patches).

modules.name: bacolonyzer.analysis
documentation: Functions to compute the outputs that will be saved.

functions.name: bacolonyzer.analysis.measure_outputs
documentation:
Add intensity measures and other measurements to a final dictionary.
This dictionary will be outputed as a data-frame.
